name: Dragon Palace
shortDescription: The Dragon Palace is an opulent underwater realm in Chinese mythology, serving as the residence of the Dragon Kings.
description: The Dragon Palace (龍宮; Lóng Gōng) is a magnificent underwater kingdom in Chinese mythology, ruled by the Dragon Kings (龍王; Lóng Wáng). It is a realm of immense wealth and power, filled with treasures, coral castles, and crystal halls. Each of the Four Dragon Kings, who govern the four seas (East, South, West, and North), resides in their own respective palace. These palaces are not merely dwellings but centers of authority, controlling rainfall, tides, and the weather in their designated regions. The Dragon Palace is often depicted as a counterpart to the celestial realm ruled by the Jade Emperor, sometimes even challenging his authority. Many stories tell of heroes and deities venturing into the Dragon Palace, either seeking powerful artifacts, negotiating for rain during droughts, or battling the Dragon Kings. The palace itself is a source of magical items and divine knowledge, but also a place of potential danger for those who dare to trespass or challenge its rulers. It is heavily guarded by various sea creatures and dragon soldiers. The Dragon Palace is a crucial element in many Chinese folktales and operas, highlighting the importance of the sea and its powerful, often capricious, rulers. The journey to and from Dragon Palace is often through a magical portal or a deep oceanic passage, accessible only to those who are destined or possess special abilities.

location.cosmological: Located beneath the seas, often described as being in a separate dimension accessible through underwater portals or deep sea passages.
